entry.title: Re: sales document type 'LKJ'
entry.content:
Hello,

To use sales document type ‘LKJ’ in Best Practice scope item 55F, you need to ensure that ‘LKJ’ is properly defined in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Here’s how you can proceed:

Steps to Define Sales Document Type ‘LKJ’

  1. Access Configuration for Sales Document Types: Configuration for Sales Scheduling Agreements

    • Navigate to the ‘Manage Your Solution’ app within your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.
    • Go to the ‘Configuration’ section to manage your sales document types.

  2. Create Sales Document Type ‘LKJ’:

    • If ‘LKJ’ is not yet defined, you will need to create it.
    • Ensure to set the correct configurations according to your business needs. For example, if the JIT (Just-In-Time) delivery is used, ensure that your configurations meet the prerequisites mentioned in related documentation.

  3. Verify Integration with Scope Item 55F:

    • Check the documentation and configuration requirements for scope item 55F to ensure the sales document type ‘LKJ’ aligns well and fulfills the necessary requirements.

  4. Testing and Validation:

    • After defining ‘LKJ’, test it within the context of scope item 55F to confirm it works as expected.
    • Validate that all business processes linked to scope item 55F function correctly with the newly configured sales document type.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Is It Standard Behavior for Zero-Quantity Lines to Ap...

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana test & demo cloud
entry.content: Hi Julia - without going too deep, I think it is a bit odd you have the license but not a contact to onboard you and your team, for example. If you could send me a private message and identify yourself and organization, I can see if there is some contact who could be helping you. Are you aware of these agreements? https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/agreements.html#partner-customer-agreements I feel there should be something more formalized for you vs just sending you to the community. Thank you, Jerry

entry.title: restrict interviewers to edit candidate rating
entry.content:
Hello community,

interviewer can edit rating even if the candidate is on the onboarding status. Any tips to restrict this option please?

entry.title: Option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM for Successful/failed TR import for third-party use
entry.content:
Hello,

We are looking for the availability of an option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM as soon as either

  • A Transport Request is successfully imported (through a Feature), or
  • A Transport Request import fails.

We want to use this event to trigger the next action in a third-party tool. Can you please help to confirm if it's possible in SAP Cloud ALM? If yes, any reference blogs/KBAs will be helpful. 
 
Thanks & Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Billing documents for clearing GLs
entry.content:
In 'Clearing between Company Codes - transaction code BUV' instead of customer and vendor, we are going to use Clearing GLs. 

I would like to know how we can create automated billing documents for the items in those clearing GLs?

entry.title: Re: Option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM for Succe...
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM does not currently offer events specific to the import status of Transport Requests in Features. The only available events related to Features are for creation, updating, and deletion of a Feature. These events contain data about the Feature itself, and not the Transport Requests that it is linked to. More Information about these events can be found in the Resource Changes for Features documentation, and information about the event payloads can be found in the Push API Resource Changes - Features documentation.

A Read API for Feature and Transport Request data is planned for Q1 2025, and may be useful in polling Features from the third party software to identify Transport Request statuses. However, this is a "Pull" API and not a "Push" API, so the API request will need to be triggered from an external source (e.g. from the third party software).

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and provide direction to our Developers in relation to this topic, please create a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation campaign on the SAP Customer Influence portal.

entry.title: Yard Logistics: Auto-Generate Opposite Yard Order does not work anymore
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have two problems:

First with the function "Auto-Generate Opposite Yard Order"

I Customized it here: 

My expectation is, that when the Inbound Yard Order is completed (manually or automatically), the system generates automatically an Outbound Yard Order. It worked well until a system update in EWM was made. Now it does not work anymore. I remember, that there is a technical queue in the backroung which must be activated. My guess is, that the queue is not active anymore. Any ideas where I find this queue to check on it? Or any other ideas why it does not work?

Second with "Auto Complete Yard Order"

Customizing:

My expectation here: The Yard Order will be completed automatically, when all Yard Tasks in the Yard Order are activated, started and confirmed. And the Outbound Yard Order will be activated, as soon as it is created. Is my expectation correct? If Yes: It does not work. If No: How is the system behaviour at this point?

 

Many thanks for your help!

Many Greetings,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Extracting Delta from S/4HANA CDS View to Datasphere...
entry.content: If you have the possibility to download the cds view in excel from the source, then also download the view from datasphere into excel and compare the primary keys. If you find the keys that are missing you might be able to find out if there is a pattern. If you are not able to figure out the problem by yourself open a ticket for CA-DI-IS-ABA.

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM API returns forbidden / 403 (in postman)
entry.content:
Hi Friscas,

You should be able to query that API based on the scopes assigned. Have you tried to use the other SAP Cloud ALM APIs? For example, I see that the scopes include the landscape permissions (calm-api.landscape.read). What happens if you try to run the "GET" method on the "/landscapeObjects" endpoint for the Landscape API?

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM API returns forbidden / 403 (in postman)

I a meeting setup tomorrow with an expert from SAP to look at this issue together, once solved it i will post a solution here. thank you a lot for your time john

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA dimension labels

They do have labels in the system, I also built one dimension with both en_US and en_GB just to check - still the same result. So the missing labels are my issue basically. Btw for department I have updated the code to "No Department" instead of "??" and you can see there are 4 of those..

User creates a goal and saves it.

But when he clicks on the goal tile on the goals page, he gets an error "You do not have permission for this action -error on goal page" 

The user is able to edit the goal, but only when he clicks it, he gets this error . Is there any action permission missing ?

Thanks,

Devendra

entry.title: Cost Center and Profit Center Hierarchy Not Appearing in "Manage Global Hierarchies" App in Fiori
entry.content:
Hello,

I created the cost center and profit center hierarchy in SAP GUI, but they are not appearing in the "Manage Global Hierarchies" app in Fiori. The Key User has requested that these hierarchies be visible in Fiori, as they are unable to view them in the Fiori interface at the moment.

I would like to know if there is any additional procedure that needs to be followed in order for the hierarchies created in SAP GUI to become visible and accessible in Fiori, or if there are any configurations or adjustments required to integrate this information correctly between SAP GUI and Fiori.

Thank you for your guidance on how we can resolve this issue.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to SOAP Integration in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello, main problem of all is : When you get the "The PayLoad elements cannot fit with the message parts of the BindingOperation" error using SOAP adapter in CPI, most likely the error is in the envelope, you need to remove it from the body, CPI automatically adds it, it would happen that there is 2x there,

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(String)
def xml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(body)
def newBody = groovy.xml.XmlUtil.serialize(xml.Body.StatusNespolehlivyPlatceRequest)
message.setBody(newBody)
return message
}

Mark

entry.title: Introducing SAP CX Observability (Dynatrace) for SAP Commerce Cloud: A Video Showcase
entry.content:
Are you ready to elevate your SAP Commerce Cloud performance monitoring? In this blog post, I will introduce SAP CX Observability (also known as Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud) – the built-in application performance monitoring (APM) tool that empowers you with deep insights and proactive troubleshooting capabilities. To help you get started and master this powerful tool, our team has curated a collection of video tutorials covering everything from basic access and essential monitoring to advanced diagnostics and digital experience management.
(Please note that some videos in this blog post may show UI components that differ slightly from the latest version of the system. Due to unpredictable SaaS updates, it's challenging to ensure every video reflects the most current version. However, the core features and functionalities remain unchanged, so you can safely disregard any minor differences.)

1. Getting Started – Basic Access

Before diving into performance metrics and analytics, it's important to know how to get into Dynatrace. Our introductory video, How to Access Dynatrace – SAP Commerce Cloud, walks you through accessing the tool within your Commerce Cloud environment. This video provides a simple, step-by-step guide to ensure you’re set up and ready to explore further.

2. Essential Monitoring

Once you’re logged in, the next step is to monitor the critical building blocks of your SAP Commerce Cloud Server. In the Essential Monitoring section, a series of videos guide you through monitoring key aspects of your infrastructure and application, including:

  • Hosts Performance – Monitor how your server hosts are performing to catch any issues before they impact user experience. Watch: Monitoring Hosts Performance in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Technologies and Processes – Understand how various technologies and background processes are operating, ensuring that your applications run smoothly. Watch: Monitoring Technologies and Processes in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Services – Delve into monitoring the performance of services, the most critical component for maintaining high availability and fast response times. Watch: Monitoring Services in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Database Monitoring – Keep an eye on your databases to quickly identify and troubleshoot performance bottlenecks. Watch: Database Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

Each of these videos builds on your knowledge, creating a comprehensive understanding of how to ensure system resilience through proactive monitoring.

3. Additional & Network/Infrastructure Monitoring

A robust monitoring strategy also requires understanding the network and infrastructure that support your SAP Commerce Cloud environment. The Additional Monitoring section focuses on:

  • Host Networking – Gain insights into the network configurations and performance that keep your systems connected. Watch: Host Networking in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Smartscape Topology – Learn how to interpret Dynatrace’s Smartscape topology to visualize and troubleshoot dependencies in your environment. Watch: Recognizing the Smartscape Topology in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

These videos highlight the importance of monitoring not just the applications themselves, but the underlying infrastructure as well.

4. Dashboard Introduction & Customization

Visualization of data is key to effective monitoring. In this section, videos guide you through the dashboards of Dynatrace:

  • Identifying Dashboards – Discover the default dashboards available in SAP Commerce Cloud and understand how to navigate them. Watch: Identifying Dashboards in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • E-Commerce Insights Dashboard – Explore a specialized dashboard designed for e-commerce insights, offering an overview of your system’s performance. Watch: Introducing the E-Commerce Insights Dashboard in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Creating and Customizing Dashboards – Learn how to tailor dashboards to your specific needs, ensuring you have the right visualizations for your operational requirements. Watch: How to Create and Customize a Dashboard in Dynatrace- SAP Commerce

These tutorials are designed to help you make sense of complex data and create reports that support decision-making.

5. Profiling, Analysis, and Troubleshooting

When issues arise, detailed analysis and troubleshooting are essential. The Profiling, Analysis, and Troubleshooting videos show you how to:

  • Perform Profiling – Identify performance issues with detailed profiling techniques. Watch: Profiling in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Continuous Memory Profiling – See how continuous monitoring of memory usage can preempt potential issues. Watch: Introducing Continuous Memory Profiling in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Multidimensional Analysis – Use multidimensional analysis to dissect web requests and uncover performance problems. Watch: Analyzing Web Requests with Multidimensional Analysis in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Troubleshoot Issues/Problems – Follow a systematic approach to identifying and resolving issues quickly. Watch: Troubleshooting Issues via Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud

These sessions provide you with the tools and techniques to diagnose and resolve problems before they affect your end users.

6. DEM – Digital Experience Management (Paid Services)

Beyond standard monitoring, Dynatrace offers DEM features to further enhance your insights into user behavior. In this section, you'll learn about:

  • Synthetic Monitoring – Understand how synthetic monitoring can simulate user interactions to test your system’s performance. Watch: Understanding Synthetic Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Real User Monitoring (RUM) – Discover how enabling RUM allows you to capture real-world user experiences, providing invaluable data for refining your systems. Watch: Enabling Real User Monitoring (RUM) in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Working with the Frontend – Explore the predefined tool set designed to support Real User Monitoring. Watch: Working with Frontend in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Session Segmentation – Understand how session segmentation can help you analyze user behavior on a granular level. Watch: Understanding Session Segmentation of Real User Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Session Replay – See how session replay offers a detailed view of user interactions, empowering you to troubleshoot and enhance the user experience. Watch: Introducing Session Replay in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

These DEM videos elevate your monitoring strategy by focusing not only on technical performance but also on end-user experience.

7. Additional Monitoring Topics

To round out your learning experience, the final set of videos covers additional monitoring topics that are essential for a comprehensive view of your environment:

  • Deployment Monitoring – Learn how to keep track of your SAP Commerce Cloud deployments and ensure continuous performance. Watch: Deployment Monitoring for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Mobile App Integration – Explore how to connect a Dynatrace subscription to your mobile applications, extending observability beyond the desktop experience. Watch: Connecting a Dynatrace Subscription to a Mobile App in SAP Commerce Cloud

Summary

These video tutorials have been carefully curated to guide you through every aspect of SAP CX Observability with Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud. Whether you're just getting started or looking to troubleshoot and improve the overall user experience, the wealth of knowledge shared in these sessions will empower you to make data-driven decisions and optimize your e-commerce operations.
Dive into the videos, explore each topic area at your own pace, and discover how SAP CX Observability (Dynatrace) can transform your approach to application performance monitoring.
We invite you to share your thoughts, questions, or success stories in the comments below
 
Happy monitoring!

entry.title: Re: Watch & Learn Microlearning Videos – SAP Variant Configuration with CPQ

entry.title: Could not build destination cache key.Isolation strategy 'tenant-user' is used.User undefined in JWT
entry.content:
Hello All,

I developed a CAPM Fiori Application in BAS. I am consuming a Standard SAP API to read the data with the help of destination in CAP service.js. It's running successfully locally. I deployed the changes to DEV and CONS. Deployment is successful but i am not getting data when i access the CAPM application in CAP FLP in DEV and CONS environment and there is no error in FLP console.

When i go to BTP subaccount and check the logs for the same application. I see the below error:

{"messageContext":"destination-cache","package":"connectivity"}, Could not build destination cache key. Isolation strategy 'tenant-user' is used, but tenant id or user id is undefined in JWT.

All the required configurations looks fine.

Kindly help me to resolve this issue.

Regards,

Mahendra

entry.title: Re: Manually editing Prices of a sales quote in SAP Cloud...
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to manually modify the pricing in a sales quote after an external price request, but it depends on the integration setup and pricing determination logic in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP ERP.

For a more standard setup, follow this link https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-q-a/add-manual-price-component-in-c4c-and-then-request-external-price/qaq-p/11414763

However, in our project, we implemented a custom SDK solution to meet specific customer needs.

We introduced a custom price field at the item level in the sales quote and contract, which is controlled via the external pricing procedure in SAP ERP. The ERP pricing procedure defines whether this field is editable or not. This information is then integrated into SAP Sales Cloud and reflected in the "Manually added/changed" column.

Since the standard pricing table is not accessible to users (it was hided), they can only modify the designated editable fields at the item level. Using SDK, we ensured that the manually edited values are transferred from the item table to the pricing table and then sent back to ERP for validation and recalculation via the simulation procedure.

This approach allows for controlled manual price adjustments while maintaining consistency with the external pricing logic in ERP.

entry.title: Re: Inactive Items Not Displayed in User Learning History...
entry.content: yes, but from the user perspective. When I proxy a user and I check his learning history plan. In prod the inactive ones are in the list, but not shown when searched with the search bar. In Test are also in the list but are also shown when are searched in the search bar.

entry.title: Re: 1st SAP Meet-Up of 2025!

entry.title: Re: How can I get an SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP BTP Extension Developer certificatio
entry.content:
Hi @FAFR 
Yes, this is retired.
Please access the SAP Certification Recommendation. This document helps you to understand how your certification will be affected with the upcoming SAP Certification program changes and what you will need to do to stay certified. 
If your certification has been retired, don't worry, you can still maintain your status by taking the recommended alternative certification exam before your current certification expires. Just check the SAP Certification finder tool, and if there is no alternative for your expiring certification, explore the portfolio for learning solutions to keep your skills relevant and your certification current beyond the expiration date.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Need help on FD10N open item warning
entry.content:
Hello,

User is trying to generate a customer report in F.27 but the report which gets generated only has 4 open items which in-turn shows incorrect balances. 

While checking the balances in FD10N I get an warning that "A large number of line items were selected" "Recommended: Only display open items. Do you want to display open items only?"

We have two options for above warning "yes or no". 

When 'Yes' is selected, I can see 39 open items.

When 'No' is selected, I can see only 4 open items. 

What is the reason behind this issue and how can we solve this? 

How can user generate the report with 39 open items which in-turn would be the correct data? 

Kindly help on this issue, Thanks.

entry.title: How to create a DB connection to HANA Data Lake in SLT system.
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to add DBCO connection for HANA Datalake in SLT system. But getting certificate issue,

Database error -10709 at CON
Connection failed (RTE:[300015] SSL certificate validation failed: SSL error [536872221]: Unknown error, General error: 0x2000051d | SAPCRYPTOLIB | SSL_connect SSL API error Failed
to verify peer certificate. Peer not trusted. 0xa0600203 |
SSL_ | tls13_handshake Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
tls13_msg_decode Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
ssl_verify_peer_certificates Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 |
SSL_ | ssl_cert_checker_verify_certificates Peer not
trusted Certificate verification failed 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
ssl_cert_checker_verify_certificates Peer not trusted -----
BEGIN VERIFICATION RESULT ----- # --- Messages -----------
ERROR: The chain of certificates is incomplete or untrusted,
missing certificate of [C1:AD:77:78] CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure
Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US # --- Summary -----------
#01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID Subject:
CN=hanacloud.ondemand.com, O=SAP SE, L=Walldorf,
SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg, C=DE Issuer:
CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US
Fingerprint (SHA256):
1B:91:75:BF:EB:78:80:EA:7A:C4:3D:1D:6F:22:29:99:9F:A1:9A:B8:
:2C:9D:C4:13:11:65:6D:7E:DC:28:43 Validity:
Tue Feb 4 19:00:00 2025 / Thu Feb 5 18:59:59 2026 PK
validation: FAILED: Validation of dependents -
Issuer Certificate (Issuer - Only Invalid Certificates
Found) #02 Certificate (Issuer): VALID Subject:
CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert
Inc, C=US Issuer: CN=DigiCert Global
Root CA, OU=www.digicert.com, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US
Fingerprint (SHA256):
C1:AD:77:78:79:6D:20:BC:A6:5C:88:9A:26:55:02:11:56:52:8B:B6:
:F5:FA:43:E1:B8:E5:A8:3E:3D:2E:AA Validity:
Tue Sep 22 19:00:00 2020 / Sun Sep 22 18:59:59 2030 PK
validation: FAILED: Validation of dependents -
Issuer Certificate (ERROR: Issuer - No Certificates
Found) ----- END VERIFICATION RESULT ----- (ErrCode:
536872221.

below is the connection string we are using.

ENCRYPTION=TLS(TRUSTED_CERTIFICATES=*;TLS_TYPE=rsa;DIRECT=yes);HOST=<SQL endpoint of Datalake>

Am i missing something? or any issue with my connection string?

entry.title: EC Promotion - Promote link in worksheet is not available
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm facing an issue when it comes to the Promotion link in the Compensation worksheets.

I've followed this information but somehow the link is not available.

Actions for All Plans

Display Settings

 

Permission Roles

The fields Job Family, Job Code, Job Role and PayGrade are included in the form (i've tested with mappings and without mappings and it doesn't work both ways). The fields are set as Read-Only and Reloadable

XML Code to Publish

Regarding the setting in Provisioning, it was probably removed because it's not available.

I couldn't validate the rest of the information because I can't test the publish.

Do you guys have any idea, suggestion?
Am I doing something wrong?

 

Thank you so much for your help

entry.title: Re: IDOC Extension

One has the standard SHPMNT05 values and the other one has our custom extension values. Checked in NACE also 1 subroutine I created is added and in partner profile also one msg type added in message control. could there be some other reason for 2 idocs generating at same time?

I thinking it must be a problem with a corrupt file somewhere on this particular environment, as it all works fine on a test platform at the same patch level

entry.title: Get certified - Live session series - An additional offering to the self-paced study - Register now
entry.content:
Our Get Certified live session series cover the most relevant topics related to the chosen SAP Certification in a series of knowledge transfer sessions. As a learner, you will receive expert-guided support to complement your self-study activities and prepare for the certification exam. 
The live sessions are an additional offering to the self-paced study, will help you better understand and apply the learned concepts.
During these sessions, you will receive tips and tricks for the exam and learn about the different question types. 

Have we inspired your curiosity - and would you  like to find out more?
Access our Get certified - Live session series for the details.

entry.title: Re: Rating ends with error which indicate wrong Charge wa...
entry.content:
Hello Dominic,

While its hard to give a clear response without checking the actual logic configured on your own SAP CC system, my initial idea is to check if the technical resources are clearly defined on both the Charge Plan responsible for Usage Consumption and also on the CIT when you create them via CIT_UPLOAD, together with the Consumption Item Class.

Furthermore, the trigger periodicity of the Recurring Rate on the other Charge Plan has to be checked, because it is unusual for a Recurring Charge to be triggered from an incoming CIT. Have you specified different Technical Identifiers/Service IDs on the Cross-Catalog-Mapping configuration interface?

Additionally, even thought the Access Denied message comes from the Recurring Rate as stated, it does not mean that the Usage Rate was not taken into account. If both Rates are executed and one leads to a No Access function, the whole Rating will fail, and unfortunately you cannot see that on the Error Message.

As a best practice, it is also advised to avoid the No Access functions on Recurring Rates, unless it is for testing purpose.

Lastly, if nothing works and you are unable to provide screenshots of the solution, you can also try to include both Recurring Charge and Usage Charge on a single Charge Plan, again by keepin in mind the above suggestions, of course, in case that this solution works for your business scenario.

Best,
Xhoi A.

entry.title: In SAP Public Cloud, how to update the currency for cost and budget, which currency should be used?
entry.content:
Dear Community,
In the Import Financial Plan Data app when I want to upload a budget or plant cost using two different currencies eg.: CHF-EUR, the exchange rate is wrong (we get 3500 CHF/3021,57 EUR, that should be 3668,35 EUR)
I tried to modify the exchange rate in the Import foreign rates  app to update the exchange rates, however, this modifications doesn't show in the Import Financial Plan Data app, amounts remain the same.

Financial data plan app might derive the exchange rates from another table.

I also tried to modify the exchange rate type in Central Business Configuration, Maintain category for planning section, exchange rate type from P to M, however, the system doesn't let me to change it.

Any insights or suggestions from your experiences would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: 0 detection objects selected for processing. - SAP BIS
entry.content: I have reviewed your issue, and I can confirm that the detection method and strategy are working as expected. The detection object data is returning a value of 0, which indicates that no alerts are generated for this detection strategy. If you believe there is still an issue, I recommend connecting with your ABAPer to review the SQL behind this strategy.

entry.title: Re: Generating OAuth Client in SAC, how do we make our te...
entry.content: Hi Martijn, I came to know that if the SAP Analytics Cloud is hosted on SAP Datacenter, we will get the Client Id and Client Secret to be specified manually. If the SAC is hosted on Non-SAP Datacenter, they are automatically generated. But now I am looking for information, token generation functionality varies based on SAP Datacenter or Non-SAP Datacenter Client ID and Secrets.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?

This doesn't seem to work:

<Button ... press="onPressFun($event , ${uiModel>/something}, 'Saved')"/>

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar | 2025 HR Trends: Turning AI Expectations into Reality
entry.content:
Now that 2025 is well underway, it’s time to take a moment to reflect on the current state of the HR function. With widespread economic volatility, significant technological disruption, and rapidly evolving workforce expectations, HR leaders are having to rethink their strategies to stay one step ahead. One topic, however, continues to make headlines with its promise to change the way people live and work – AI. While there’s no shortage of AI excitement, many HR leaders still face uncertainty around what it truly means for their business and their employees. There’s no question that AI has potential, but how can organizations move beyond hype to make it a reality?

To help cut through the noise so you can unlock real value from AI, we’re thrilled to be hosting a webinar with Kathi Enderes, Senior Vice President Research and Global Industry Analyst at The Josh Bersin Company. Kathi will join Dr. Autumn Krauss, Chief Scientist at SAP SuccessFactors, for an engaging discussion on 2025 HR trends and the impact AI is driving in organizations of all sizes.

During this session, we’ll cover:

  • The latest HR trends and what they mean for your organization
  • Key insights and recommendation for putting AI to work in 2025
  • The priority AI use cases and the measurable impact organizations are realizing

Register here to secure your spot or use the following links to sign up for your local webcast:

  • NA and LATAM time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 11:00 AM EDT
  • APJ and GC time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 1:00 PM SGT
  • EMEA and MEE time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 11:00 AM CEST

We look forward to seeing you there!

Best Regards,

Neenu

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - Node - Fiori List Report: How to refresh Li...
entry.content:
I resolved the problem.

For unbound action it should be so:

service.cds:

service Service {

@Common.SideEffects: { TargetEntities: ['/Service.EntityContainer/app'] }
action Unbound();

entity app as projection on my.App;
}

entry.title: Re: Milestone Billing - SAP SD
entry.content: As of S/4HANA 2502 Public Cloud on Enterprise Projects milestones have moved from just the project to WBS elements. These milestones still cannot generate the billing plan on the Sales Order but that is on the roadmap for Q1 2026.

entry.title: Re: Milestone Billing - SAP SD
entry.content: MarkoPa,  you could look into developing a BPA on BTP to read the WBS milestones and write them to the Sales Order Item Billing Plan.

entry.title: Re: Prepare for the Future: Major SAP SuccessFactors Changes Coming in 2025 and 2026
entry.content:
Hi @nageshpolu,

Is Workforce Planning still available or is it being phased out?

Regards,
Sean

entry.title: Has anyone received one of the below error messages in Onboarding 2.0 and do you know how to resolve
entry.content:
These two errors are occurring when initiating onboarding in 2.0.

This new hire created their candidate profile and entered their entire phone number in the area code field.  The profile has been updated but the application the candidate is being hired on has not been updated.  The phone number is a required field when creating the candidate profile and we don't know how they got around that.  

An error occurred while saving the HRIS data for the user XXXX due to the following reason: phoneInfo[C].phone-number field can’t be empty.

This new hire created their candidate profile and did not provide their country code.  The profile has been updated but the application the candidate is being hired on has not been updated.  The country code is a required field when creating the candidate profile, we are still investigating how this happened.

An error occurred while saving the HRIS data for the user XXXX due to the following reason: {"responsePayload":[{"userSysId":"111586","hrisSaveStatus":[{"elementType":null,"key":null,"statusCode":"ERROR","errorInfo":"Invalid Country: -1 in entity: homeAddress for UserId: 111586"}]}]}.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Intake Management: A Seamless, User-Centric Solution with Powerful Extensibility
entry.content:
The currently the SAP Ariba Intake Management is in limited availability and available to select set of early adopter customers. General Availability (GA) is planned for early Q3 2025. To explore early adopter enrollment, please contact your SAP AE or the Finance & Spend Sales Specialist assigned to your account.

For partners, the TDD slated to be available with the Learning Journey launching around GA timeframe 

Partners interested in early adopter activities should reach out to their respective Partner Business Manager.

entry.title: PLM Tech Uncovered: SAP Integrated Product Development Update 03/2025
entry.content:
🆕  Welcome to the first edition of my new blog series which brings concise updates about SAP Integrated Product Development’s monthly releases straight from Engineering directly to you!

The latest update in March 2025 brings the following top highlights for your business:

Formulation
Regional Flexibility for Formulas: Customize recipe calculations based on local country or regional regulations, ensuring compliance. 

Visualization
Digitalizing Legacy Drawings: Convert existing PDFs and 2D drawings into interactive assets, adding clickable hotspots to make them more manageable and informative.

Specification Management
Compare Specifications with Visual Differences: Quickly see differences between product specifications with visual highlights, improving speed and reducing mistakes.

💡  Want to get all the details?
➡️  Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer and check out our Roadmap Explorer.

📰  Or just continue reading to find out what else is new:

Requirements Management
AI Assistance for Writing Requirements: Speed up the process of writing requirements with AI-driven improvements, helping to create more accurate and complete documents faster (currently in beta).

Formulation
Flexible Recipe Calculations: Gain greater control over recipe calculations, making it easier to manage and display them as needed. 
Plant-Specific Configurations: Link recipes to specific manufacturing plants to enhance efficiency and flexibility.
Advanced Formula Calculations: Improve recipe accuracy with more precise calculation capabilities. 
Drag-and-Drop for Formula Maintenance: Easily reorder formula items using a simple drag-and-drop interface. 

Enterprise Product Structure
Multilingual Support: Support global teams with product structures translated into multiple languages for better collaboration.

🔔  See you again for the next update in April 2025! In the meantime, don’t forget to:

➡️  Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer on SAP Integrated Product Development Help Portal 
🗺  Check out the Roadmap Explorer for SAP Integrated Product Development 
🌐  Visit us at www.sap.com/PLM 
🤝  Join our LinkedIn group SAP Solutions for PLM

Email: patryk.waledziak@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com

Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: SAP Business Integrity Screening Overview
entry.content:
SAP Business Integrity Screening (BIS): An Overview & Implementation Guide
 
Introduction
 
In today’s business landscape, fraud detection, compliance monitoring, and risk management are critical. SAP Business Integrity Screening (BIS) is a powerful solution that helps organizations identify suspicious transactions, prevent fraud, and ensure regulatory compliance in real time.
 
In this blog, I’ll cover:
✔ What is SAP BIS?
✔ Key Features & Benefits
✔ How BIS Works (Architecture & Process Flow)
✔ Implementation Steps in SAP S/4HANA
✔ Use Cases & Real-World Applications
 
By the end, you’ll have a clear understanding of SAP BIS and how to implement it in an SAP environment.
 
⸻
 
What is SAP Business Integrity Screening (BIS)?
 
SAP BIS is an advanced fraud detection and risk management tool within SAP S/4HANA. It helps businesses:
• Identify and prevent fraudulent transactions in real-time
• Improve regulatory compliance (e.g., AML, GDPR, SOX)
• Reduce financial losses due to fraud
• Automate screening using predictive analytics & machine learning
 
SAP BIS is part of the GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) suite, ensuring organizations remain compliant while proactively mitigating risks.
 
⸻
 
Key Features of SAP BIS
 
🔹 Real-time Monitoring – Detects suspicious activities as they occur
🔹 Rule-Based & Predictive Analytics – Uses AI/ML to enhance fraud detection
🔹 Integration with SAP & Non-SAP Systems – Supports multiple data sources
🔹 Alert & Case Management – Automates fraud investigation workflows
🔹 Flexible Customization – Configure fraud detection rules for different industries
 
SAP BIS is widely used in finance, banking, insurance, retail, and supply chain operations.
 
⸻
 
How SAP BIS Works (Architecture & Process Flow)
 
SAP BIS follows a 3-step process:
 
1. Data Collection & Preprocessing
 
✔ SAP BIS collects data from multiple sources (SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA, external databases)
✔ Transactions are preprocessed & indexed using SAP HANA
 
2. Screening & Fraud Detection
 
✔ Data is screened using rule-based models & machine learning algorithms
✔ Identifies suspicious patterns (e.g., duplicate payments, money laundering, vendor fraud)
✔ Generates alerts & fraud risk scores
 
3. Case Management & Investigation
 
✔ High-risk transactions are flagged for review
✔ Integration with SAP Case Management allows fraud analysts to investigate
✔ System provides audit trails & reports for compliance purposes
 
Technical Stack: SAP HANA, SAP Fiori, SAP GRC, Machine Learning
 
⸻
 
Step-by-Step Implementation of SAP BIS in S/4HANA
 
1. Activate BIS in SAP S/4HANA
• Use SAP GUI → Transaction Code: SPRO
• Go to: Governance, Risk, and Compliance → Business Integrity Screening
• Enable BIS configurations
 
2. Configure BIS Screening Rules
• Define fraud detection rules based on business needs
• Example: Identify duplicate vendor payments
 
IF InvoiceAmount > $100,000 AND VendorCountry = ‘High-Risk’ THEN Flag as Suspicious
 
• Use ML models for anomaly detection
 
3. Integrate with Data Sources
• Connect BIS with SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, SAP Ariba, External APIs
• Configure batch jobs for continuous screening
 
4. Set Up Alerts & Case Management
• Use Fiori Apps for Fraud Case Handling
• Configure automated alerts for risk analysts
 
5. Test & Optimize
• Run test cases with historical transaction data
• Fine-tune detection thresholds to minimize false positives
 
⸻
 
Use Cases & Real-World Applications
 
✔ Banking & Finance: Detect fraudulent transactions & money laundering
✔ Retail: Identify fraudulent customer refunds & loyalty point abuse
✔ Supply Chain: Spot vendor collusion & invoice fraud
✔ Healthcare: Prevent insurance fraud & claim manipulations
 
⸻
 
Conclusion
 
SAP Business Integrity Screening is a powerful tool that enhances fraud detection and compliance efforts in SAP S/4HANA environments. By rule-based analytics, organizations can prevent financial losses, reduce risks, and automate compliance monitoring.
 
🔹 Let me know your thoughts in the comments!

entry.title: Sales rebate processing - condition contract pricing
entry.content:
Hi all,

we need to have additional condition types in sales condition contracts, so those were created and customized but they are not visible when creating a condition contract. 

The pricing procedure is customized and also assigned to sales contract type 0S01, the condition table only contains the field "condition contract number" so there is no link to the condition types. Why are the new condition types not available when creating a condition contract?

Thanks and best regards

entry.title: Re: Optimize your Accruals in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: The link at the end doesn't work  "Sorry, no search results were found" can you check?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud -Master Form Template changes not showing up in the related Content Forms
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (TEST) - The updates and changes I've made in my Master Form Templates continuously are not showing up in the related content forms. 

 

For example, our company created a customer master form during original implementation that had no footer. No this is something we very much want to utilize. I identified the correct master form, downloaded it, updated it in Adobe LiveCycle, uploaded it. My assumption is now when I download the related content form it should should display this footer but it doesn't. Am i missing something? perhaps there is an additional step?

Similarly, we don't want any "Test Copy" watermarks in any of our forms in any situation. So I updated several master forms to delete the watermark object and binding. I than did the same process as above, but again none of the content forms show these changes. 

Please help! We really want to bring these changes into production. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to deploy a Vite Reactjs App into SAP BTP Trial a...
entry.content:
Hi,

1. Create new vite react-ts project:

pnpm create vite my-react-app --template react-ts
cd my-react-app
pnpm i

2. Create manifest.yaml:

---
applications:
  - name: vite-test
    path: ./dist
    instances: 1
    buildpack: staticfile_buildpack
    memory: 256M

 3. Build and deploy

pnpm build
cf push

Comments:
With staticfile_buildpack you can provide static files and do not rely on build process inside buildpack.

entry.title: Re: Why the expiry date of SF "Certified Professional" in...
entry.content:
Hi @imlty00 

The specific verbiage as it relates to SAP SuccessFactors Professional level certification is pasted below for quick reference:

Please be aware that if you hold an SAP SuccessFactors Professional level certification, it will not appear on the SAP Learning site. However, your current Professional badge remains valid as long as you have fulfilled the Stay Certified requirement. After the March 31 deadline, all Professional certifications will be reviewed and extended. To keep your Professional credential, make sure to complete the Stay Certified course before the March 31 deadline.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: ChangeHistory.Read for Contacts - unable to read email address changes
entry.content: we found a workaround:

entry.title: Re: ChangeHistory.Read for Contacts - unable to read email address changes
entry.content:
In order to read the contact person's email change history, you need to pass the RELATION UUID, not the account UUID or contact UUID

var contactParty = this.MainContactParty;

if (contactParty.IsSet()) {

var contactID = contactParty.PartyID.RemoveLeadingZeros();

var contactInst = BusinessPartner.Retrieve(contactID);

var firstRel = contactInst.BusinessPartnerRelationship.GetFirst();

var changes = ChangeHistory.Read("AP.FO.BusinessPartnerRelationship.Global:BusinessPartnerRelationship", firstRel.UUID);

}

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba User Provisioning Using SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS)
entry.content: @Francois-Xavier This is currently built for non FPC Ariba realms. There should not be any difference in setting up for the FPC where parent realm receives the users and parent realm to child realm can create partition users. We can extend the concept mentioned in this blog to FPC realms. Hope this helps.

Margit

entry.title: Re: Reactive maintenance order w.r.t notification error (...
entry.content:
I just wanted to share the cause of this error. Here's what I found out after research and help from SAP support:

In case of reactive maintenance on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public edition, it is mandatory that the technical object should have a valid work center. We need to assign work center in organizational data tab of equipment and functional location. Work center automatically gets copied from the master data of functional location and equipment while creating a reactive maintenance order. While accepting the maintenance request in Screen Maintenance Request App, make sure to look into change responsibility function and see if there's any work center mentioned. If not, enter a relevant work center there in order to avoid any issues while creating the reactive maintenance work order.
Thanks

entry.title: Re: Using Default Route transit time in the Freight Unit scheduling
entry.content:
Hello,

Are those dates transferred correctly to the freight order? The calculation works correctly for us in the freight unit, but when planning it in the freight order, it breaks down.

entry.title: Freight Order/Booking is disassociated from Freight Unit and Delivery
entry.content:
Greetings,

Many of our FUs are being removed from planned and confirmed freight orders and bookings. This behavior is frequently observed when ATP is retrigged by SAP TM delivery proposals and manual updates to the order schedule line delivery date field. We hypothesize that ATPs inability to confirm inventory after these events is triggering a change controller method (REMOVE_TOR_FROM_PLANNNG) within SAP TM which removes TORs from planned assignments. As expected, this behavior is causing many inefficiencies within planning and execution as well as confusion on the carrier side. 

To address this, I need guidance on how to configure SAP TM to prevent the disassociation of FUs from planned assignments all the while providing visibility to "non-compliant "orders, in this case, orders lacking inventory confirmations post ATP check. I've reviewed the following SAP Knowledge Base Article but it doesn't provide specifics on how to address the matter systematically - 3085710 - Freight Order/Booking is disassociated from Freight Unit and Delivery - SAP for Me. 

Assuming that this is change method controller issue - Is there a way to reconfigure change control method "REMOVE_TOR_FROM_PLANNNG"? Do we need to configure other change methods to override the mentioned change method?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
I have the same issue as @lorenzo_nicora2

If I modify the source code and I a press "pretty printer" or "Activate" before saving, all unsaved changes are lost.

Is there already a solution/note for this issue ?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP BODS/IS upgrade from 4.2 SP14 to 4.3 SP03
entry.content:
@MekhaMahendraprabhath 

I would recommend going with the latest IPS/DS/IS releases.

  1. Download IPS, DS and IS from
    • For IPS:
      • SAP Software Centre > Support Packages & Patches > By Category > Analytics Solutions > SAP Data Services > SAP Data Services 4.3 > SBOP Information Platform SVCS 4.3 > 
      • Filter by the Operating system
      • Download SBOP Information Platform Services 4.3 SP04 Patch1000 ONE (IPS4304P_1000-70002777.TGZ)
    • For DS:
      • SAP Software Centre > Support Packages & Patches > By Category > Analytics Solutions > SAP Data Services > SAP Data Services 4.3 > SAP Data Services 4.3
      • Filter by the Operating system
      • Download SBOP DATA SERVICES 4.3 SP03 PL 4 HF2 (DS4302H_42-80007395.TGZ)
    • For IS:
      • SAP Software Centre > Support Packages & Patches > By Category > Analytics Solutions > SAP Information Steward > SAP Information Steward 4.3 > SAP Information Steward 4.3 > SAP INFORMATION STEWARD 4.3
      • Filter by the Operating system
      • Download SAP INFORMATION STEWARD 4.3 SP 03 PATCH 4 (IS4303P_4-80007399.TGZ )
  2. Make sure you have BI/IPS/DS/IS 4.3 License Key before starting upgrade.
    • For IPS License key, please open a ticket in the component XX-SER-LIKEY-BOJ and request IPS License key
  3. Take backups of the CMS repository
  4. The rest of the steps are pretty much the same steps you follow when patching or upgrading. Make sure to update IPS license key 4.3 in CMC > License Keys after upgrading IPS and Enable the CMS Servers (CMC > Servers > Server List), otherwise, you will not be able to proceed with DS & IS upgrade.
    • Information platform services Installation Guide for Unix
    • SAP Data Services - Installing without uninstalling
    • SAP Information Steward - Upgrade an existing environment
  5. After Upgrading all the components, Upgrade DS & IS Repositories
  6. Resync the repositories with the Job Server
  7. Validate that everything works.

Regards,

Joe

DRC Report shows Minus sign for Box 3

 

Tax Box Structure you can see Tax Group 3 is assigned with Transaction MWS which is output.

entry.title: How to modify Invoice Template
entry.content:
Good day,

We are moving to Euro  (Bulgaria) and we need to modify the template for our invoices. I have been talking with Accounting, but they are not sure how this is done. I did some research by myself, and I did find out that it probably needs to be done via the T-CODE "NACE" - Can someone help me with this? I cannot seem to find the correct way to modify the template, and my Accounting team has not been really helpful, as their SAP Systems were set up by Hicron years ago.

Can someone guide me throughout the process to modify the template for our invoices?



All the best,
Nikola

entry.title: How to Peek into Queues in SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
entry.content:
In this short blog post, we will explore how to leverage JMSToolbox to connect to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM), and peek at events that are in a queue.

JMSToolbox

First of all, what is JMSToolBox? JMSToolbox is a JMS Client that allows us to easily connect to different Queue Managers/Queue available.

We can download JMSToolbox straight from the project's GitHub repository: https://github.com/jmstoolbox/jmstoolbox. We will need to download the binaries from the repository. Installation instructions may vary depending on the platform on which you will be running it.

AEM connection details

Before we can connect to AEM via JMSToolbox, we will need some details from our event broker service.

  • Broker connection details for the Solace Messaging protocol. Go to the Event Broker service you want to connect to > Connect tab > View by protocol and expand the Solace Messaging accordion. Select any language from the list, and the credentials should appear on the right side. From here, we need the host URI, username, and password.

  • Event broker service management details. We need the service management URL, VPN, username, and password. In the same event broker service go to the Manage tab > expand the Broker Manager - Web application accordion. The required details should appear on the screen. See the screenshot below.

     

Connecting to AEM in JMSToolbox

Now that we have the AEM connection details, we can proceed to configure JMSToolbox.

In JMSToolbox we create a new session and configure a connection to AEM. Here you will input the Session Name, Queue Manager, Host/Port (from the Solace Messaging connection details), and Security User id and password. The connection details will look similar to the ones in the screenshot below.

Before creating the session we will define some properties. Enter the event broker service management details in the respective fields - VPN, mgmt_url, mgmgt_username, mgmt_password, and then click the Create button. The session properties will look similar to the ones in the screenshot below.

Once configured, it will connect to AEM and automatically show all the queues available in the event broker service. Click on any queue to peek at the events available.

Messages in Queue

I hope this quick blog post help you troubleshoot/explore events in your AEM queues. You can learn more about JMSToolbox and AEM brokers (Solace) here.

entry.title: Re: Key User Custom Fields in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise: Dos and Don’ts
entry.content: Great blog post Karsten! Especially the dos and don'ts in the context of software logistics also apply to other key user extensions like Custom Business Object or Custom Logic.

entry.title: Condition contract management set different document number ranges for accruals credit memos
entry.content:
Hello,

Is it possible to set different credit memos number ranges when using accruals, such that one number range is not transferred to financial accounting e.g. for accruals and the other real credit memo (e.g. created at partial and final settlements) has another number range which is then transferred to financial accounting?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Is there a scope item which support the Intracompany...
entry.content: Hi Jose, thanks for your answer, I have others quick questions, regarding to "Enterprise Management Layer" option, how we can enable this template? Does this template imply an additional cost or it is already included for S4 Hana Private Cloud? We will require this functionality not only for testing or demo purpose also for production implementation. Thanks for your comments

entry.title: Re: Can a manager be able to edit a performance form even if it is not in his/her performance inbox
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: Past 1 week fr 17-Mar-2025 i am waiting for my SAP Le...
entry.content:
Thank you @Margit_Wagner .

SAP Store Support team responded yesterday (25-Mar-2025) and they have forwarded my request to process my ticket under "Individual" account, as per my request. I am waiting for an update from SAP Store Support team.

Meganathan Vellore Vedaram.

entry.title: Re: Need help on FD10N open item warning
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

I think that the difference that you notice is due to the selection of open items.

If you don't get the same number of open items, it might be because one of the selection include special G/L transactions linked to the customer.

Could you please check this ?

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: PO workflow approval for PPV
entry.content:
Are you using PPV as a purchase group or do certain material groups belong to that identification on the PO?-- if you are in S/4 you can make use of a custom workflow or RPA that would able to trigger the email --

You can also explore if there is anything available in Situation Handling on Purchase orders that can send alerts for specific criteria that are met.

If not on S4 then you can use ME2N set up as a batch job to send the POs meeting the specific criteria at a defined frequency of the job.

entry.title: Re: I need to change the tax rate set on the purchase ord...
entry.content: Hola rogerio_lima, gracias por tu respuesta. la liquidaciones de fletes ya creadas asi quedaran con el primer impuesto, pero para las posteriores, donde puedo cambiar para que automaticamente tome mi nuevo impuesto?
